recording_notes: Phone filming Insignia monitor (different location from RCA lab). Black suture, 1920x1080. Indirect camera angle partially obscures loop geometry on first throw.

frame_analyses.description: First instrument visible, approaches Penrose for needle orientation.
Both instruments engaged orienting needle for bite.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Several regrasping maneuvers to optimize angle.

frame_analyses.description: Suture pulled through Penrose after bite; tails separated across field.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth pull-through, drain fixed, no avulsion.

frame_analyses.description: First knot throw with broad loop around post near center of slit.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Best estimate double-wrap first throw; angle partially obscures loop count.

frame_analyses.description: First throw seated with both instruments pulling in opposition.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Controlled seating with wide excursions and extra motion.

frame_analyses.description: Opposing single throw passed and tightened over first throw.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: More efficient and flatter than first.

frame_analyses.description: Third throw advanced with crossover/regrasping near knot stack before final seating.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Knot centered; repeated regrasping lengthens phase.

frame_analyses.description: Intracorporeal tail trimming begins with multiple repositioning attempts.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Slowest segment; heavy contributor to total time.

frame_analyses.description: Both suture tails cut short; instruments withdraw, knot seated on Penrose.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Stable final knot stack, no obvious loosening after release.

knot_assessments.notes: Right-led surgeon's knot best estimate; wrap count partially obscured by indirect angle.
Left-led opposing single; seats flatter with less excess loop.
Right-led single; additional regrasping/locking manipulation before cutting.

drain_assessment.assessment_note: No visible gap after tightening or cutting; Penrose anchored.

technique_summary: Needle passage close to intended marks; drain remains fixed throughout with no visible avulsion or gapping after tying. Main time loss: broad loop construction, repeated regrasping during later knot sequence, prolonged intracorporeal cutting. Final knot stack stable.
improvement_suggestions: Reduce loop size and keep a more consistent post during knot construction.
Transition to cutting sooner after final throw is seated; stabilize one tail more firmly.
Manage tail length more deliberately after suture placement to reduce regrasping before each subsequent throw.
strengths: Needle placement close to both target marks with only small estimated deviation.
Penrose anchored with gentle tissue handling, no avulsion.
Final knot stack seated with short tails and no loosening after withdrawal.
comparison_notes: Strong agreement with Claude V22 (ΔFLS 2.5). Both teachers call this a solid, technically sound run limited by motion economy. Total time converges within 5s (Claude 152 vs GPT-4o 157). Placement penalties slightly different (Claude 3.0 vs GPT-4o 2.5). Drain closure agreement. Phase-level disagreement on where throws end: Claude's third_throw closes at 98s, GPT-4o's at 124s (absorbs more of the late manipulation). Both flag cutting as the primary score-improvement lever.
